stages.label: Source
stages.description: The encoder at the venue sends a live RTMP or SRT feed — this is where the stream begins.

stages.label: Ingest
stages.description: The ingest point receives the feed and hands it off to the processing platform (AMPP).

stages.label: AMPP
stages.description: AMPP processes, transcodes, and routes the stream. This is where Live Ops monitors bitrate and stream health.

stages.label: Packager
stages.description: The packager segments the stream into HLS or DASH chunks for adaptive bitrate delivery.

stages.label: CDN
stages.description: The CDN (Content Delivery Network) distributes the stream globally, getting it close to viewers.

stages.label: Viewer
stages.description: The viewer's player requests and plays back the stream, adapting quality based on their connection.
